task_completion.description: Navigate to the user or account management section in FreeScout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeScout web interface displaying a list of users or the user management settings.

task_completion.description: Ensure the recently active user remains active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user 'active.recent@helpdesk.local' is visible in the user list and has an 'Active' status (or lacks any 'Inactive'/'Deactivated' badge).

task_completion.description: Ensure the borderline active user remains active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user 'active.borderline@helpdesk.local' is visible in the user list and has an 'Active' status (or lacks any 'Inactive'/'Deactivated' badge).

task_completion.description: Deactivate the user who has not logged in for over 90 days without deleting them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user 'dormant.old@helpdesk.local' is still present in the system (not deleted) but is clearly marked with an 'Inactive' or 'Deactivated' status badge or toggle.

task_completion.description: Deactivate the user who has never logged in without deleting them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user 'dormant.never@helpdesk.local' is still present in the system (not deleted) but is clearly marked with an 'Inactive' or 'Deactivated' status badge or toggle.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeScout web GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the FreeScout web application in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, API calls, or scripts to update users.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database queries to update user statuses.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows with MySQL/PostgreSQL prompts or database administration tools are visible modifying the users table.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open or used to manipulate the DOM to fake 'Inactive' badges.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The deactivation threshold is strictly 90 days. Users that MUST be deactivated (set to inactive, not deleted): dormant.old@helpdesk.local (180 days inactive) and dormant.never@helpdesk.local (never logged in). Users that MUST remain active: active.recent@helpdesk.local (5 days inactive) and active.borderline@helpdesk.local (80 days inactive).
